notes:
Didn't work last week and doesn't work now. Game does not run, just launches a box that says "Fatal D3D Error (1)" then the game quits. Tried multiple proton versions (experimental, git from last night, 7.0.6, 7-43 ge 7-50, hotfix). Mesa version doesn't seem to matter. Re-downloaded. Verified files.

I don't get how this is a "gold" rating. These ratings mean nothing anymore. A good number of reports here have setup issues, performance issues or straight doesn't run. Does gold just mean runs the same as Windows? XP

May be my GPU but I didn't swap out to check.

notes:
Tested on the specs listed. Was getting horrible stutters until I ran a few commands to force my card's clocks up.

echo performance > /sys/class/drm/card0/device/power_dpm_state

echo manual > /sys/class/drm/card0/device/power_dpm_force_performance_level

echo 1 > /sys/class/drm/card0/device/pp_power_profile_mode

title: Resident Evil 4

notes:
When I first installed this a month ago, I played the first 5 hours flawlessly, no issues whatsoever. I then did a fresh install to Fedora 37 from 36 and upon doing so was updated to the newer drivers and the game started crashing every time I tried to load my game. No amount of tinkering has changed anything.

Another earlier report mentioned that the problem seems to be tied to driver versions for Nvidia, reporting 515 or older to be working. I think this is correct, but am unable to test it.

title: Resident Evil 4

notes:
TLDR - Intel Arc DOES NOT SUPPORT "sparse" memory in Vulkan, and is what causes the "Fatal D3D error (1)" before the game even gets to a menu. You can not run RE4 with Intel on Linux as of mid 2023.

You can check the git issues and pages but basically they do not have support, and seemingly do not plan to backport support, for the "sparse" Vulkan extensions required to play RE4 Remake. There is a newer Kernel driver, "xe", which will replace the long living "i915" driver for gen11/12+. However they also seemingly have no interest in supporting HuC (read: video codecs or hardware acceleration) in the new driver (which I would assume will affect some type of media/cutscene playback, mfplat or some other headache). There are reports that the new Kernel driver (highly experimental as of writing) and new Mesa (also dev/beta and multiple patches as of writing) will run, but it seems like Intel is either way behind, or not very interested in Linux GPU support.
title: Resident Evil 4
